parsed_review.summary: CP00-437 is a descriptor-only settlement-core closeout pack (RP14 program, range RP14.P04.M04.S20-RP14.P05.M02.S09, 150 units, risk class C) closing RP14.P04 (M04 tail through M10) and opening the RP14.P05 foundation (M00-M02 head). It follows the established, well-tested CP426-CP436 descriptor-only pattern across registry.js, service.js, validators.js, the settlement contract, the validator script, README, and the pack manifest/command-evidence. Review focus areas all check out: (1) production_ready is correctly NOT asserted - manifest.json and command-evidence.json both report production_ready:false / runtime_ready:false with status implemented_pending_review; the descriptor only carries production_ready_candidate (internal coverage validity), and final closeout is gated behind this review plus post-review commands that are correctly deferred. (2) Deliverable distribution counted directly from the baseline source plan (implementation 40, ui 51, claude_review 14, security_audit 12, fixture 23, test 5, hermes_evidence 5 = 150) matches the requirements constants, the contract, and the manifest exactly. (3) Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer and not final approver: no_write_attestation.promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false, descriptor authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false and human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true; all enforced in validators and asserted in tests. (4) Hermes control-plane patterns are confined to deterministic descriptor evidence packet/receipt handling (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, no_hermes_runtime_receipt=true; hermes_packet is a descriptor-only evidence shape). (5) no-write/no-real-data boundaries are extensively enforced (synthetic_only, no_real_data, writes_product_state=false, plus the full no-runtime/no-leak guard set) and tested. CP178+ review-hardening blockers (failed_authentication_run, zero_byte_output, malformed_json_output, fenced_json_output, tool_call_shaped_output, plus claude_final_approval_claim / source_mutation_claim / production_enterprise_trust_claim) are all present in forbidden_review_evidence. The auth preflight in readiness-report.json is genuine (passed, exit 0, 1443-byte valid JSON result, is_error:false), not a forbidden pattern. No premature or fabricated review artifacts exist (no claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json, raw-output.json, or review-receipt.json yet) - the correct pre-review state. All pre-review validation commands recorded exit_code 0. One non-blocking P3 note on redundant scope-file cataloging. Verdict: PASS; does not block pack or goal closeout.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json lists the pack's own evidence dir and handoff doc in both untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved
findings.details: docs/closeout-packs/cp00-437/ and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md each appear in both untracked_implementation_files (lines 130-133) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 135-145). A path cannot simultaneously be a pack implementation artifact and unrelated. This is benign cataloging redundancy in the scope-generation metadata: the pack's commit scope is driven by active_pack_implementation_refs (the 8 settlement files), and the manifest's authoritative worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved correctly excludes the pack dir and handoff doc, listing only the genuinely unrelated files (the v2.0 docx, the workbook md/zip/index files, and the ldip invalid-attempt json). No unrelated file is swept into the pack and no boundary is affected.
findings.recommendation: Optionally de-duplicate the scope generator so a pack's own evidence directory and handoff doc are not also emitted under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved. Non-blocking; no action required for CP00-437 closeout.
